equivalence_argument: No complete mapping was found: the current RPC path performs one pool submission and returns the error immediately; retry constants and sleeps are absent from audited repos.

negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing submitDeliveryResult retry loop of 10 pool lookups with 100 ms sleeps before not-found failure.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/ethereum/node/src/ritual_async_rpc.rs builds AsyncDeliverySubmission and calls pool.submit_async_delivery_result once, then returns Err as success=false.
Only rpc_compat tests contain unrelated retry constants for node syncing, not submitDeliveryResult pool lookup.
Invariant 201 in this same batch maps the replacement single-lookup behavior.

rationale: The commit message describes a race where Phase 2 delivery can arrive before Phase 1 finalization inserts the pool entry, and the diff adds MAX_POOL_RETRIES, RETRY_DELAY_MS, repeated async sleeps/lookups, and only fails after retries. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: When submitDeliveryResult cannot immediately find the target transaction in the async pool, it must retry the pool lookup up to 10 times with 100 ms delays before returning a not-found failure, and must proceed if the transaction appears during a retry.
suggested_falsification_tests: Mock the pool so get_async_transaction_by_hash returns None initially and Some after several calls; assert submitDeliveryResult waits/retries and succeeds. Also mock permanent None and assert it returns success=false only after exhausting 10 retries.
summary: Delivery-result RPC lookup retries are not present in the current audited repos.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Mock an initially absent async pool entry that appears after several lookups; current local submitDeliveryResult should still fail after the first pool submission path.
title: Delivery result submission retries pool lookup
